+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "alertmanager-2250-kubernetes-peers-ip-caching-causes-all-clusters-to-degrade-ove",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "alertmanager: [Kubernetes] Peers ip caching causes all clusters to degrade over  time",
+    "description": "[Kubernetes] Peers ip caching causes all clusters to degrade over  time. Requested by 5+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current alertmanager deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your alertmanager version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager\nhelm list -n alertmanager 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working alertmanager installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review alertmanager configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant alertmanager configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager\nkubectl get configmap -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=alertmanager\n```\n**What did you do?**\n\nWe have deployed several different `Alertmanager`'s - in different namespaces\n\n**What did you expect to see?**\n\nEach alertmanager installation forms a separate cluster\n\n**What did you see instead? Under which"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [Kubernetes] Peers ip caching causes all clusters to…",
+        "description": "Can you elaborate about your setup? Which names do you use for `--cluster.peer`?\nAlertmanager will try to reconnect to a previously known IP address for 6 hours by default. After this, it will forget about it.\n```yaml\n- args:\n    - --config.file=/etc/alertmanager/config/alertmanager.yaml\n    - --cluster.listen-address=[$(POD_IP)]:9094\n    - --storage.path=/alertmanager\n    - --data.retention=120h\n    - --web.listen-address=:9093\n    - --web.external-url=https://alertmanager-address\n    - --web.route-prefix=/\n    - --cluster.peer=alertmanager-main-0.alertmanager-operated.namespace.svc:9094\n    - --cluster.peer=alertmanager-main-1.alertmanager-operated.namespace.svc:9094\n    - --cluster.peer=alertmanager-main-2.alertmanager-operated.namespace.svc:9094\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager\nkubectl get events -n alertmanager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"[Kubernetes] Peers ip caching causes all clusters to…\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Can you elaborate about your setup? Which names do you use for `--cluster.peer`?\nAlertmanager will try to reconnect to a previously known IP address for 6 hours by default. After this, it will forget about it.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "- args:\n    - --config.file=/etc/alertmanager/config/alertmanager.yaml\n    - --cluster.listen-address=[$(POD_IP)]:9094\n    - --storage.path=/alertmanager\n    - --data.retention=120h\n    - --web.listen-address=:9093\n    - --web.external-url=https://alertmanager-address\n    - --web.route-prefix=/\n    - --cluster.peer=alertmanager-main-0.alertmanager-operated.namespace.svc:9094\n    - --cluster.peer=alertmanager-main-1.alertmanager-operated.namespace.svc:9094\n    - --cluster.peer=alertmanager-main-2.alertmanager-operated.namespace.svc:9094",
+        "$ kind create cluster\n...",
+        "$ helm install my-release foo/bar\n$ helm install my-bad-release foo/bar"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
